---
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

<div class="gradient-text">
<div class="welcome-headline">
The Experience Machines
</div>
<div class="welcome-subheadline">
Modeling Moral Attachments in Art
</div>
</div>

::::{.cmm-background style="display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; min-height: 85vh; background-size: cover; background-position: center;"}
::: {.content-container style="max-width: 1200px; width: 100%; padding: 2rem 1rem;"}

<!-- TOP BOX -->
::: {.welcome-text .sand-background style="padding: 2.5rem; margin-bottom: 2rem; text-align: left; border-radius: 8px;"}
**Philosophy for the Real World**

The Moral Imagination and Hope Workshop and Laboratory is a group of artists, academics, and other creative types interested in understanding how the art we consume shapes who we become. <em><strong>The Experience Machines</strong></em> is a <em>philosophical laboratory</em> where we invite the public into virtual worlds to explore these issues with us.

:::

<!-- FOUR BOXES – using Quarto grid classes -->
::: {.grid .gap-4 .mb-4}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-3}
::: {.welcome .sand-background style="padding: 1.75rem; height: 100%; border-radius: 8px;"}
**Art’s capacity to ignite ethical reflection**
on our deepest bonds—family, friendship, love—and the meaningful paths toward union with God.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-3}
::: {.welcome .sand-background style="padding: .75rem; height: 100%; border-radius: 8px;"}
**Interactive Ethics**
How video games create moral dilemmas that force players to confront their values in ways traditional philosophy cannot.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-3}
::: {.welcome .sand-background style="padding: 1.75rem; height: 100%; border-radius: 8px;"}
**Music & Moral Identity**
How artists use narrative to help listeners process emotions and imagine ethical commitments.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-3}
::: {.welcome .sand-background style="padding: 1.75rem; height: 100%; border-radius: 8px;"}
**Aesthetic Sentimentalism**
Bridging aesthetics and ethics to understand how beauty, emotion, and moral knowledge intertwine in popular art forms.
:::
:::
:::

<!-- BOTTOM BOX -->
::: {.welcome .sand-background style="padding: 2.5rem; text-align: left; border-radius: 8px;"}
**Join us** we are always looking for:

* Links to papers (past or present) on music, identity, games and ethics
* Reviews of games with interesting approaches to ethical play
* Reviews of books on games, music and identity
* Announcements of events (conferences, workshops etc.) related to art and identity.
:::

:::
::::
